plan_metadata.name: Feature Add App With Unsupported Feature From TDP

steps.description: Use the keyboard shortcut **Ctrl + `** to open the integrated terminal in the Visual Studio Code workspace welcome screen.
Type the text `open https://dev.teams.microsoft.com` into the integrated terminal (bash) at the bottom of the Visual Studio Code workspace.
Press the Enter key in the integrated Terminal of Visual Studio Code to execute the command: open https://dev.teams.microsoft.com.
Enter "${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}" into the email or username input field on the Microsoft "Sign in" page at login.microsoftonline.com.
Press Enter while the email address input field is focused on the Microsoft sign-in page at login.microsoftonline.com, triggering the "Next" button action.
Click on the "Password" input field on the Microsoft login page to focus the cursor for entering a password.
Type the password '${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}}' into the "Enter password" field on the Microsoft account sign-in page for user in a Chrome browser window.
Press the Enter key in the password input field on the Microsoft login page to submit the entered password for the user.
Click the "No" button in the Microsoft "Stay signed in?" dialog on the login.microsoftonline.com sign-in page.
Click the "App features" menu item in the Configure section of the left sidebar navigation within the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal web interface.
Reduce the zoom level of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface by pressing Ctrl and minus (-) while viewing the "App features" section under the "Configure" menu for the app.
Reduce the zoom level in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal web interface by pressing Ctrl and minus (-) while viewing the "App features" section for the app.

Zoom out the "App features" page in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal by pressing the keyboard shortcut Ctrl + -; this reduces the UI scale and provides a wider view of feature options being configured for the app.

Click the "Activity feed notification" feature card in the "App features" section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal to select it for adding to your app. The click targets the bottom-most card under "Select a feature to add," highlighted by the red crosshair.
Click the "Create your first activity feed notification" button in the Activity feed notification section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal.
Type text 'aaa' into the "Type" input field in the "Add an activity feed notification" dialog within the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal web application.
Click the "Description" input field in the "Add an activity feed notification" dialog of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal to activate text entry.

Type text 'aaa' into the "Type (unique Graph API call identifier that isn't visible to users)" input field in the "Add an activity feed notification" dialog within the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal web application.
Click the "Title" input field labeled "Enter a title" in the "Add an activity feed notification" popup on the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Enter text 'aaa' into the "Title (for example, {actor} assigned you {workItemId})" input field in the "Add an activity feed notification" dialog within the Developer Portal for Microsoft Teams.

Click the "Confirm" button in the "Add an activity feed notification" dialog within the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal web application. The button is located at the bottom right of the popup form, visually styled in blue.
Click the "Save" button at the bottom center of the Activity feed notification page in Microsoft Teams Developer Portal.
Click the "Open in Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" link in the left sidebar under the "Develop" section within the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal web interface.
Click the "Open in Visual Studio Code (JS/TS)" button in the Develop section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal web application.
Click the "Open Visual Studio Code URL Handler" button in the browser popup dialog requesting permission to open Visual Studio Code from the Developer Portal application.
Click the language selector (currently showing "en" and flag icon) at the bottom left corner of the VS Code welcome screen to open language options.
Click the "Open" button in the confirmation dialog prompting to allow the 'Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit' extension to open a URI in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Sign in" button on the Microsoft 365 login prompt within the Visual Studio Code interface, located at the top center of the screen.
Click the "Use weaker encryption" button in the OS keyring warning dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Password" input field on the Microsoft login screen in a web browser to activate text entry for account authentication.
Type text "${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}}" into the password field on the Microsoft login page for user.
Click the blue "Sign in" button on the Microsoft login page to submit the entered password and proceed with authentication.
Click the browser window's "Close" button (red X) in the top-right corner to exit the "Visual Studio Code – You are signed in now and can close this page" tab.
Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the "New Project" dialog within Visual Studio Code, under the extension for Microsoft 365 Copilot.
Click the "No Action" option in the "Create Declarative Agent" dropdown in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default folder" option in the "Workspace Folder" selection dialog within the Visual Studio Code interface to select the project root directory.
Press the Enter key to confirm the input in the application name text field at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
@assertion "App contains unsupported features" exists.
